finding: Core vote-EV model shows no demonstrated edge at scale

atClosingPrices.withLiveCalibration: negative at every threshold (train -29% to -100%, test -19% to -43%)

atOpeningPrices.note: tests the early-flag proposition the live tool actually runs

interpretation: Positive CLV with negative ROI: the model picks sides the market drifts toward slightly, but they still lose more often than their price implies.

The earlier +14.5% out-of-sample result that justified evThreshold 0.08 came from a 674-game sample with only n=27 bets. On 9,381 games with n=153-313 bets the sign flips negative. That earlier validation was itself underpowered.
Live record (+10u over 95 settled picks) is consistent with variance, not edge — live avg CLV is only +0.60 probability points and just 33% of picks beat the close.

contrast.driftCrowdSignal: On this SAME 9,381-game panel the drift-crowd signal held up: train n=164 roi +6.1%, frozen test n=110 roi +16.8%. It remains the only component with positive large-sample evidence.

actionTaken: Core model flags labelled UNPROVEN on the dashboard rather than presented as value bets.
Promotion gate unchanged: drift-crowd must still earn 50 live settled bets with positive roi and CLV before it drives flags.
No threshold re-tuning — tuning a strategy with negative large-sample expectancy is how the earlier mistake happened.
